benchmark_window.description: For each scored row at fiscal year Y, the benchmark window is the set of available years in [Y-6 ... Y]. Rolling per-EIN, not a fixed global window.
benchmark_window.scoring_year_notes: FY2023 is fully populated (41,822 CA+WA EINs). FY2024 is 61% complete (26,766 EINs) due to IRS processing lag — usable but flagged.
benchmark_window.top_quartile_rule: Benchmark metrics are evaluated within cohort-year and traced through the documented fallback hierarchy.
benchmark_window.covid_note: FY2020-2021 are inside the window for FY2023-2024 scoring. PPP/EIDL inflated margins and runway. Median-based benchmarks dampen this naturally, but implementations should be aware.

confidence_tiers.High: Six to seven years present and less than 20 percent missing across the fixed key fields.
confidence_tiers.Medium: Four to five years present, or fallback cohorts / relaxed benchmark rules with otherwise usable filings.
confidence_tiers.Low: Three or fewer years present, more than 20 percent missing key fields, broadest-cohort fallback, or insufficient resilient references.

action_labels.Amplify: Top-quartile resilience profile within cohort, stable or improving trend, and no severe stress-test failure.
action_labels.Stabilize: Below benchmark but recoverable, with positive recovery analog evidence and a fixable financial profile.
action_labels.Diversify: Acceptable operating health with elevated concentration risk or stress-test sensitivity.
action_labels.Deep Review: Structurally weak profile, very low confidence, or no clear near-term intervention path.

recovery_analogs.source_pool: Full national panel (all states), not limited to CA+WA. 1,278 analogs validated in EDA.
recovery_analogs.cohort_filter: Same ntee_major_category + size_bucket as the target org. State-agnostic. State shown in output for context.
recovery_analogs.selection_rule: Prefer same cohort first, then relax to size_bucket only if strict cohort yields zero analogs.
